ClaimProvenanceOut.description: One claim with its evidence — the unit the provenance panel renders.
ClaimProvenanceOut.title: ClaimProvenanceOut

GradingOut.description:
The three dimensions, kept apart (Article III).

There is deliberately no combined score here. A single number would be the
one thing every caller reached for, and it cannot be reconstructed back
into the judgements that produced it.
GradingOut.title: GradingOut

GraphEdgeOut.description:
One time segment of one predicate — never a collapsed span (ADR-030).

There is no ``weight``. ``support`` carries each visible claim's three
grading dimensions and the corroboration/contradiction counts around them,
so a reader can reach the evidence instead of trusting a scalar.
GraphEdgeOut.title: GraphEdgeOut

GraphExpandIn.description:
A bounded traversal request (specs/06 §2.6).

Every bound is clamped rather than rejected (specs/06 §4): a client asking
for six hops gets three and is told the result was truncated, which is more
useful than a 422 that teaches nothing about the limit.
GraphExpandIn.title: GraphExpandIn

IdentityDecisionOut.description: A human's identity decision: who, when, why, and which revision.
IdentityDecisionOut.title: IdentityDecisionOut

MentionOut.description: The words a claim's argument came from (ADR-029).
MentionOut.title: MentionOut

ProjectionStampsOut.description: Which build produced these rows, and whether it is behind (specs/06 §3).
ProjectionStampsOut.title: ProjectionStampsOut

WhyConnectedOut.description: The answer to GOAL.md §18 for one pair of entities.
WhyConnectedOut.title: WhyConnectedOut

get.description:
Generic provenance for any claim-derived value (B-14).

Property values, aliases and edge labels all resolve to a claim, so one
route serves every "where did this come from?" in the UI.

The decision line behind an entity: who merged or split it, when, why.

Not gated on claim visibility: a decision records an adjudication, not the
content of any source. Its note is written by the reviewer for exactly this
audience (Article V, specs/06 §2.2).
Every claim this caller may see connecting two entities, with evidence.

post.description:
Breadth-first expansion from seeds, or the bounded overview without them.

POST rather than GET because the request is a query object — seed sets,
category filters and time windows — not a resource address, and putting a
hundred entity ids in a query string is how URL-length limits become silent
truncation of an authorization-relevant input.

Shortest routes between two entities, bounded by hops and count.

A path here is a claim about *what the evidence connects*, not about what
anyone did: every edge on it opens onto its claims, and none of them is
graded by its position on the path (Article IX).
